sources.note: Login-gated — refresh suno-v55-genre-wheel.js manually when Suno updates the wheel.

metaTags.name: Track
metaTags.meaning: Defines the overall track container and sets global control parameters for the entire song. This tag is placed at the very start of the track definition and governs attributes such as genre, style, mood, length, instrumentation, and other high-level defaults.

metaTags.name: Accelerando
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a gradual increase in tempo, creating a sense of urgency, excitement, or transition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[tempo]`, `[structure]`, or `[rhythm]`. Accepted Parameters: gradual - Slow, steady increase in speed.

metaTags.name: Ad Lib
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a freely improvised vocal or instrumental phrase, often used in solos or embellishments.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: vocal - Spontaneous, expressive singing.

metaTags.name: Ambient
metaTags.meaning: Defines a track as atmospheric, textural, and non-rhythmic, often using drones, pads, or environmental sounds.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[style]`, `[mixing]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: textural - Emphasizes layered sound textures.

metaTags.name: Announcer
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a section header or inline role cue: `[announcer: retro radio DJ, warm, friendly tone]`

metaTags.name: Aria Rise
metaTags.meaning: A section marked by an operatic, rising vocal phrase, often dramatic and soaring.
metaTags.placement: Within `[vocals]` or `[structure]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   solo – single dramatic vocal.

metaTags.name: Arpeggio
metaTags.meaning: Defines a broken chord sequence, where notes are played individually instead of simultaneously.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[rhythm]`. Accepted Parameters: rising - Arpeggio pattern ascends.

metaTags.name: Arrangement
metaTags.meaning: Defines the organization of musical elements, including instrumentation, section order, and layering.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[orchestration]`, or `[mixing]`. Accepted Parameters: dense - Full, layered orchestration.

metaTags.name: Articulation
metaTags.meaning: Specifies how notes are played in terms of attack, transition, and connection between them.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[instruments]` or `[style]`. Accepted Parameters: staccato - Short, detached notes.

metaTags.name: Attack
metaTags.meaning: Defines how quickly a note reaches its full volume after being played.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[dynamics]`, `[instruments]`, or `[mixing]`. Accepted Parameters: sharp - A quick, percussive attack (good for plucked strings, electronic leads).

metaTags.name: Background Vocals
metaTags.meaning: Specifies harmonized or complementary vocals that support the lead vocal.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[harmony]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: harmonic - Background vocals provide harmonic support.

metaTags.name: Bass
metaTags.meaning: Defines the bassline characteristics, its prominence, and style.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[instruments]`, `[mixing]`, or `[rhythm]`. Accepted Parameters: deep - Low, rumbling bass frequencies.

metaTags.name: Bass Slide
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a sliding bass note, often used for groove or tension.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[bass]`, `[rhythm]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: upward - Slide from a lower pitch to a higher note.

metaTags.name: Beat Switch
metaTags.meaning: Indicates a sudden or gradual change in rhythm, often altering tempo, groove, or drum patterns.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]`, `[structure]`, or `[break]`. Accepted Parameters: sudden - A sharp, unexpected change in beat.

metaTags.name: Big finish
metaTags.meaning: Defines a grand, climactic ending, often with increased volume, instrumentation, or dramatic buildup.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[orchestration]`. Accepted Parameters: orchestral - Full symphonic ending.

metaTags.name: Break
metaTags.meaning: A brief pause or stripped-down section in the song, often used for tension or transition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[rhythm]`, or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: instrumental - A solo instrumental section.

metaTags.name: Breakdown
metaTags.meaning: A section where the instrumentation is stripped down, usually reducing intensity before building back up.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[rhythm]`, or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: percussive - Emphasis on drums or rhythmic elements.

metaTags.name: Bridge
metaTags.meaning: A contrasting section that connects different parts of a song, often providing harmonic or melodic variation.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: melodic - Focus on a new melody line.

metaTags.name: Build
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a section start tag, often combined with descriptive modifiers: `[build | rising tension, more drums, more synth layers]`

metaTags.name: Cadence
metaTags.meaning: Defines how a musical phrase ends in terms of chord progression or resolution.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[harmony]`, `[structure]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: perfect cadence - Strong resolution (V-I progression).

metaTags.name: Cadential
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the type of harmonic progression leading into a cadence (the ending of a musical phrase or section).
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[structure]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: strong - A definitive cadence, often V-I (dominant to tonic).

metaTags.name: Call And Response
metaTags.meaning: Defines a musical interaction where a phrase (the "call") is followed by a responding phrase (the "response"), commonly used in blues, gospel, jazz, and African music.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[structure]` or `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: instrumental - The response is played by instruments.

metaTags.name: Chant
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a repetitive, rhythmic vocal phrase, often used for emphasis or ritualistic effect.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[structure]`, or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: repetitive - The phrase is looped multiple times.

metaTags.name: Chant Loop
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a repeated vocal or instrumental chant, looped for trance-like or ritualistic effect.
metaTags.placement: Often within `[vocals]`, `[structure]`, or `[texture]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   ritual – repetitive, tribal-like chants.

metaTags.name: Choir
metaTags.meaning: Specifies group vocal harmonization, often for a grand, emotional effect.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[harmony]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: layered - Thick, multi-part harmony.

metaTags.name: Chorus
metaTags.meaning: Defines the main repeated hook of the song, often the most memorable part.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: anthemic - Big, singalong chorus.

metaTags.name: Chromatic
metaTags.meaning: Refers to the use of notes outside the diatonic scale, creating richer harmonic movement and tension.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: ascending - A chromatic passage moving upwards.

metaTags.name: Climax
metaTags.meaning: Defines the peak moment of intensity in the composition, usually involving increased dynamics, energy, or harmonic tension.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: sudden - A sharp, unexpected burst of intensity.

metaTags.name: Cluster
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the use of tone clusters—closely spaced notes played together to create a dissonant or textured sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: soft - Gentle, ambient clusters (e.g., softly played piano or synth pads).

metaTags.name: Coda
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the concluding section of a piece, often distinct from the main body, used for closure or reinforcement of themes.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: recapitulative - Revisits earlier themes before resolving.

metaTags.name: Compression
metaTags.meaning: Defines dynamic range processing, used to balance loud and soft parts in a track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: light - Soft compression, retaining dynamic variation.

metaTags.name: Control
metaTags.meaning: Specifies how certain elements of the composition are structured, processed, or restricted.
metaTags.placement: Typically used at the beginning of the definition, as it applies to global track parameters. Accepted Parameters: instrumental - No vocals in the track.

metaTags.name: Consonance
metaTags.meaning: Specifies harmonies that sound stable, resolved, and pleasant, in contrast to dissonance.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[chords]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: soft - Warm, gentle consonance (e.g., major 3rds, perfect 5ths).

metaTags.name: Content
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the lyrical or thematic focus of the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[lyrics]`, `[theme]`, or `[mood]`. Accepted Parameters: narrative - A storytelling-driven approach.

metaTags.name: Counterpoint
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the interweaving of multiple independent melodic lines, often used in classical and complex compositions.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: simple - Light counterpoint with two melodies.

metaTags.name: Crescendo
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a gradual increase in volume and intensity, building anticipation and emotional impact.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[dynamics]`, `[structure]`, or `[orchestration]`. Accepted Parameters: slow - A long, drawn-out build-up.

metaTags.name: Development
metaTags.meaning: Defines the evolution of a theme, whether through variations, new harmonies, or instrumental shifts.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: thematic - The original theme evolves over time.

metaTags.name: Diminuendo
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a gradual decrease in volume and intensity, opposite of a crescendo.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[dynamics]`, `[structure]`, or `[orchestration]`. Accepted Parameters: slow - A long, gradual decrease.

metaTags.name: Dissonance
metaTags.meaning: Refers to harmonic tension created by unstable or clashing note combinations. Often used to create drama, tension, and emotional intensity.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[chords]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: mild - Subtle tension, often used in jazz or cinematic music.

metaTags.name: Distorted vocals
metaTags.meaning: Applies a gritty, overdriven, or processed effect to the voice. Produces a raw or industrial tone.
metaTags.placement: Within `[vocals]` or `[effects]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   harsh – strong distortion, almost screamed.

metaTags.name: Distortion
metaTags.meaning: Specifies audio processing that adds harmonic saturation, clipping, or fuzz to an instrument or sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]`, `[mixing]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: light - Gentle distortion for warmth (often in blues, rock).

metaTags.name: Drop
metaTags.meaning: Defines a sudden shift in intensity, often a key element in EDM and modern music styles.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: bass-heavy - A deep, sub-bass-driven drop.

metaTags.name: Drum Fill
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a short percussive passage that serves as a transition between sections.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]`, `[structure]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: simple - A short, clean drum fill.

metaTags.name: Duet
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* At the start of the track or specific sections: `[duet | female lead + male lead, alternating lines]`

metaTags.name: Dynamics
metaTags.meaning: Defines how volume and intensity change over the course of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[structure]`, or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: soft-loud - Gradual build from quiet to intense.

metaTags.name: Echo
metaTags.meaning: Defines delayed repetitions of a sound, creating a sense of space and depth.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[effects]`, `[mixing]`, or `[sfx]`. Accepted Parameters: short - Quick, tight echo (adds rhythmic texture).

metaTags.name: Effects
metaTags.meaning: Specifies additional sound effects applied to instruments, vocals, or the overall track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[sfx]`, or `[instruments]`. Accepted Parameters: reverb - Adds spaciousness to the sound.

metaTags.name: Element
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a specific musical component to emphasize in the mix.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[focus]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: melody - Focus on lead melodic instruments.

metaTags.name: Emotional
metaTags.meaning: Defines the emotional tone of the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mood]`, `[style]`, or `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: melancholic - Sad, reflective tone.

metaTags.name: End
metaTags.meaning: Defines the final section or conclusion of a piece.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[coda]`, or `[outro]`. Accepted Parameters: sudden - An abrupt stop.

metaTags.name: Ensemble
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the type and size of musical group performing the piece, ranging from small acoustic duos to full orchestras.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]`, `[orchestration]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: duo - Two performers or instruments.

metaTags.name: Epic
metaTags.meaning: Defines a grand, large-scale, and dramatic quality in the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[style]`, `[orchestration]`, or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: cinematic - Large orchestral arrangements, often used in film scoring.

metaTags.name: Episode
metaTags.meaning: Defines a self-contained musical section, often functioning as a contrasting or transitional moment.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[form]`, or `[development]`. Accepted Parameters: melodic - The episode introduces a new melody.

metaTags.name: Eq
metaTags.meaning: Defines equalization settings, adjusting frequency balance for clarity or effect.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[effects]`, or `[instrument]`. Accepted Parameters: bass-heavy - Emphasizes low-end frequencies.

metaTags.name: Exposition
metaTags.meaning: The first introduction of the main musical theme, often recurring in various forms throughout the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: thematic - Clearly introduces the song's main theme.

metaTags.name: Extend Style
metaTags.meaning: Defines the way an extension of the track is handled, particularly in Suno's extension feature.
metaTags.placement: Typically used at the beginning of the "Lyrics" input, as it applies to how the track is extended beyond its original length. Accepted Parameters: seamless - The extension continues smoothly from the original.

metaTags.name: Fade
metaTags.meaning: Specifies how the track gradually reduces volume, often at the end or between sections.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[outro]`. Accepted Parameters: slow - Gradual volume decrease over an extended period.

metaTags.name: Female
metaTags.meaning: Specifies that the lead or backing vocals should be female.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[harmony]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: high - Soprano or high-pitched female vocals.

metaTags.name: Fermata
metaTags.meaning: Indicates that a note or chord is held longer than its usual duration, creating a moment of emphasis or suspense.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: sustained - A long hold on a note.

metaTags.name: Finale
metaTags.meaning: Defines the grand, concluding section of a composition, often marked by increased intensity or a resolution.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[coda]`, or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: grand - A strong, full-orchestra or multi-instrument climax.

metaTags.name: Focus
metaTags.meaning: Specifies which instrumental, rhythmic, or harmonic elements should be most prominent in the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: melody - Emphasis on the lead melodic line.

metaTags.name: Fragmentation
metaTags.meaning: Refers to breaking down a melodic, harmonic, or rhythmic theme into smaller motifs or segments, which are then repeated, altered, or developed.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[theme]` or `[variation]`. Accepted Parameters: melodic - A fragmented version of the main melody.

metaTags.name: Fugue
metaTags.meaning: Defines a counterpoint-based composition where a theme is introduced and then developed through layered entries.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: subject - The main theme, stated clearly at the beginning.

metaTags.name: Gain
metaTags.meaning: Adjusts relative loudness/emphasis of a section or instrument layer.
metaTags.placement: Section-based; best in `[intro:]`, `[climax:]`, or `[bridge:]`. Accepted Parameters: `low`, `medium`, `high` - Relative section gain.

metaTags.name: Genre
metaTags.meaning: Defines the overall stylistic category of the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically at the start of the definition, as it determines how the track is structured. Accepted Parameters: single-genre - A single, well-defined musical genre (e.g., `[genre: jazz]`).

metaTags.name: Glissando
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a continuous slide from one pitch to another, often associated with string instruments, trombones, synths, or harps.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]` or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: ascending - The pitch slides upwards.

metaTags.name: Glitch
metaTags.meaning: Specifies artificial, digital-style distortions that contribute to a fractured or unpredictable sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[effects]`, `[structure]`, or `[rhythm]`. Accepted Parameters: stutter - Repetitive chopping of audio.

metaTags.name: Grind
metaTags.meaning: Defines aggressive, fast-paced, or heavily distorted instrumentation, often used in extreme genres.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[mixing]`. Accepted Parameters: metallic - Sharp, industrial-style distortion.

metaTags.name: Happy
metaTags.meaning: Defines the emotional tone of the track, ensuring that the composition conveys joy, positivity, and energy.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mood]`, `[style]`, or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: bright - A cheerful, lighthearted mood.

metaTags.name: Harmonics
metaTags.meaning: Refers to overtones produced above a fundamental pitch, common in strings, brass, and woodwinds. They create an airy or shimmering sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]`, `[harmony]`, or `[style]`. Accepted Parameters: natural - Harmonics produced by lightly touching a string.

metaTags.name: Harmony
metaTags.meaning: Defines how chords and multiple voices interact, emphasizing harmony-driven elements in the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[structure]`, or `[arrangement]`. Accepted Parameters: simple - Basic, diatonic harmonies.

metaTags.name: Hook
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a section label or inline cue: `[hook] Wherever we go, the record spins slow.`

metaTags.name: Improvisation
metaTags.meaning: Allows performers to freely create melodic, harmonic, or rhythmic variations during performance.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[style]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: freeform - Fully spontaneous improvisation.

metaTags.name: Inflection
metaTags.meaning: Refers to subtle tonal or dynamic variations that alter expressiveness.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[dynamics]`, or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: subtle - Gentle variations in pitch or dynamics.

metaTags.name: Instrument
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a particular instrument that is prominent in the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]`, `[arrangement]`, or `[focus]`. Accepted Parameters: piano - Emphasizes piano as a lead instrument.

metaTags.name: Instruments
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the instruments used in the composition, helping define the timbre and orchestration of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed before structure tags to establish instrumentation at the beginning of the track definition. Accepted Parameters: Specific instruments - e.g., piano, violin, electric guitar, synthesizer, brass, flute, harp.

metaTags.name: Instrumental
metaTags.meaning: Ensures that the track contains no vocals, focusing entirely on instrumentation.
metaTags.placement: Typically used at the beginning of the "Lyrics" section to indicate that no lyrics should be generated. Accepted Parameters: orchestral - Fully symphonic arrangement.

metaTags.name: Intensity
metaTags.meaning: Controls emotional/musical tension and arc of energy throughout the song.
metaTags.placement: Can be placed globally or per section. Highly effective in `[control:]` or `[sequence:]` aligned use. Accepted Parameters:

metaTags.name: Interlude
metaTags.meaning: Defines a short instrumental break between sections, often used to provide contrast or transition between verses and choruses.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed between structured sections such as `[verse]`, `[chorus]`, and `[bridge]`. Accepted Parameters: instrumental - Purely instrumental passage.

metaTags.name: Intermezzo
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a self-contained musical passage that may be unrelated to the main sections but serves as a brief contrast.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed between primary musical sections like `[movement]`, `[theme]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: contrasting - A section that differs from the main theme.

metaTags.name: Intro
metaTags.meaning: Defines the opening section of a track, setting the mood, instrumentation, and rhythm before transitioning into structured sections.
metaTags.placement: Typically at the beginning of the track definition. Accepted Parameters: soft - Gentle, understated opening.

metaTags.name: Inversion
metaTags.meaning: States that a theme or motif should be inverted (played upside-down in melodic contour).
metaTags.placement: Paired with `[subject]` or `[motif]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   strict – precise inversion.

metaTags.name: Lament
metaTags.meaning: Defines a sorrowful, descending motif or expressive passage.
metaTags.placement: Within `[melody]`, `[harmony]`, or `[vocals]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   descending – typical lament bass line (e.g. minor descending tetrachord).

metaTags.name: Language
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the language of the lyrics or vocal performance in the generated track.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed at the beginning of the track definition or before `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: English - Standard default setting.

metaTags.name: Laughter
metaTags.meaning: Adds laughter as a sound effect or vocal element in the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[sfx]`, `[vocals]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: subtle - Light chuckles, background effect.

metaTags.name: Layering
metaTags.meaning: Defines the use of multiple sounds, instruments, or textures stacked together to create a thicker or more dynamic sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[orchestration]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: dense - A full, rich arrangement with multiple instruments layered.

metaTags.name: Legato
metaTags.meaning: Specifies smooth, connected note transitions in melodies and harmonies.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: soft - Gentle legato phrasing.

metaTags.name: Length
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the desired duration of the track, controlling overall runtime.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed before `[structure]` to ensure Suno processes it before structuring the composition. Accepted Parameters: short - 30-60 seconds.

metaTags.name: Male
metaTags.meaning: Specifies that the lead or backing vocals should be performed by a male voice.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]`, `[harmony]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: low - Deep bass or baritone male vocals.

metaTags.name: Male vocal
metaTags.meaning: Specify dominant vocal gender identity.

metaTags.name: Marcato
metaTags.meaning: Indicates that notes should be played in a forceful, accented manner.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]` or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: sharp - Sudden, pronounced attack on each note.

metaTags.name: Minuet
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a dance-like, triple-meter (3/4 or 6/8) structure reminiscent of classical minuets.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[style]`, `[rhythm]`, or `[tempo]`. Accepted Parameters: baroque - Authentic minuet style from the 17th-18th century.

metaTags.name: Modulation
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a change in key or tonal shift within the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[structure]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: `sudden`, `abrupt` - An abrupt key change for dramatic effect.

metaTags.name: Mood
metaTags.meaning: Defines the emotional atmosphere of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used at the start of the track definition to guide composition style. Accepted Parameters: dark - Brooding, mysterious, or ominous.

metaTags.name: Mutation
metaTags.meaning: Refers to the transformation of a melody, harmony, or rhythm into a new variation over time.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[theme]`, or `[variation]`. Accepted Parameters: gradual - The transformation happens slowly.

metaTags.name: No
metaTags.meaning: Negates or disables a particular feature or characteristic in the track. It is used to explicitly prevent the inclusion of certain musical elements.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed before structure or vocal tags to ensure exclusions are processed early. Accepted Parameters: no-vocals - Ensures the track is fully instrumental.

metaTags.name: No Repeat
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* At the start of a section or directly before important lines: `[no-repeat | verse with simple, one-time images]`

metaTags.name: Orchestra
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the type of orchestral arrangement used in the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed before `[instruments]` or `[structure]` to define the orchestration. Accepted Parameters: symphonic - Full orchestra with all sections.

metaTags.name: Orchestration
metaTags.meaning: Defines how instruments are arranged and distributed in the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[ensemble]`, `[structure]`, or `[style]`. Accepted Parameters: dense - A rich, full orchestral arrangement.

metaTags.name: Outro
metaTags.meaning: Defines the final section of the track, specifying how it concludes.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed after `[structure]`, indicating the closing style. Accepted Parameters: fade-out - Gradually decreasing volume to silence.

metaTags.name: Pad
metaTags.meaning: Defines the type of sustained, atmospheric background sound layers used to enhance depth.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]`, `[structure]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: warm - Soft, analog-style pads.

metaTags.name: Pedal Point
metaTags.meaning: A sustained or repeated note, typically in the bass, that remains constant while harmonies above it change.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[bass]`, or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: low - A deep, rumbling pedal note in the bass.

metaTags.name: Personae
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the vocal character or persona performing the track, ensuring consistency across verses, harmonies, and vocal layers.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed before `[vocals]` or `[harmony]`, defining a character or vocal identity early in the track. Accepted Parameters: gritty-male - A deep, textured male voice.

metaTags.name: Pizzicato
metaTags.meaning: Defines plucked string articulations for a more rhythmic and staccato effect in string instruments.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[instruments]` or `[harmony]`. Accepted Parameters: light - Subtle, delicate pizzicato.

metaTags.name: Polyphony
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a section descriptor, especially for choirs or group singing: `[chorus | polyphony, overlapping choir lines, call-and-response]`

metaTags.name: Power Off drop
metaTags.meaning: Defines a dramatic, abrupt silence effect, simulating an electronic system suddenly turning off.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]`, `[drop]`, or `[effects]`. Accepted Parameters: sudden - Instant drop with no reverb tail.

metaTags.name: Pre Chorus
metaTags.meaning: Defines the section leading from the verse into the chorus, building tension and anticipation.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed between `[verse]` and `[chorus]` in `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: rising - Gradual build-up into the chorus.

metaTags.name: Prelude
metaTags.meaning: Specifies an introductory musical passage, often setting the mood for the entire composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before `[structure]`, `[intro]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: solo - A single instrument introduction.

metaTags.name: Pronunciation
metaTags.meaning: Specifies how words should be enunciated in the vocal performance, which can be useful for ensuring clarity or stylization.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[vocals]` or `[language]` to ensure it is applied correctly. Accepted Parameters: clear - Words are pronounced distinctly.

metaTags.name: Pulse
metaTags.meaning: Defines the rhythmic foundation or heartbeat of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]`, `[tempo]`, or `[structure]` to guide the groove. Accepted Parameters: steady - A constant, unchanging pulse.

metaTags.name: Quiet arrangement
metaTags.meaning: Defines a section with minimal instrumentation and reduced dynamics, emphasizing sparseness or intimacy.
metaTags.placement: Within `[arrangement]` or `[structure]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   ambient – airy pads, little motion.

metaTags.name: Rapped verse
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a rap-delivered verse, rhythmically spoken instead of sung.
metaTags.placement: Within `[vocals]` or `[structure]` for verses. *   Accepted Parameters: *   fast-flow – quick delivery.

metaTags.name: Recapitulation
metaTags.meaning: Refers to the return of the main theme, often after a development section, commonly used in classical and cinematic compositions.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[structure]` or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: exact - Identical to the original theme.

metaTags.name: Refrain
metaTags.meaning: Defines a repeated phrase or musical passage, typically appearing in multiple sections of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within `[structure]`, `[chorus]`, or `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: melodic - A tune repeated throughout.

metaTags.name: Register
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the pitch range where a melody or harmony is played.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[orchestration]`. Accepted Parameters: low - Deep bass registers.

metaTags.name: Resolution
metaTags.meaning: Defines how musical tension is released, often through chord progressions or phrase endings.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[structure]`, or `[cadence]`. Accepted Parameters: strong - A definitive resolution (e.g., V-I in classical music).

metaTags.name: Retrograde
metaTags.meaning: Indicates a melody, motif, or harmony played in reverse.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[theme]`, `[variation]`, or `[counterpoint]`. Accepted Parameters: melodic - The notes of the melody are played in reverse order.

metaTags.name: Reverb
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the level and type of reverberation, controlling depth and space in the track’s mix.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[effects]`, `[mixing]`, or `[vocals]`. Accepted Parameters: dry - Minimal or no reverb.

metaTags.name: Reverberate
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a strong and continuous reverb effect, creating an echoing, immersive atmosphere.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [effects], [mixing], or [vocals]. Accepted Parameters: soft - Gentle, subtle reverb.

metaTags.name: Rhythm
metaTags.meaning: Defines the rhythmic pattern and overall groove of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [tempo], [pulse], or [structure]. Accepted Parameters: steady - Even, predictable rhythm.

metaTags.name: Rhythmic Motif
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a recurring rhythmic pattern or idea that defines the groove of a piece.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]`, `[structure]`, or `[theme]`. Accepted Parameters: syncopated - Offbeat rhythmic patterns.

metaTags.name: Ritardando
metaTags.meaning: Indicates a gradual slowing down of tempo, commonly used in classical, cinematic, and dramatic music.
metaTags.placement: Typically placed within [tempo], [structure], or [dynamics]. Accepted Parameters: gradual - Smooth, drawn-out slowing.

metaTags.name: Riff
metaTags.meaning: Defines a recurring instrumental phrase, typically for guitars, bass, or synths.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure] or [theme]. Accepted Parameters: heavy - Distorted, strong rock/metal riff.

metaTags.name: Rise
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a buildup or gradual increase in energy, often used before a climax or drop.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], [build-up], or [dynamics]. Accepted Parameters: slow - Gradual, long buildup.

metaTags.name: Rondo
metaTags.meaning: Defines a musical structure based on a recurring main theme (A) alternating with contrasting sections (B, C, etc.), typically structured as ABACA or ABACABA.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], [form], or [theme]. Accepted Parameters: classical - Traditional rondo form as found in sonatas and concertos.

metaTags.name: Sad
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a melancholic or emotional mood for the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [mood], [dynamics], or [harmony]. Accepted Parameters: melancholic - Gloomy, sorrowful tone.

metaTags.name: Scale
metaTags.meaning: Defines the musical scale or mode used in the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [harmony]. Accepted Parameters: major - A bright, uplifting scale.

metaTags.name: Scat break
metaTags.meaning: Denotes a scatting vocal improvisation section, usually jazz-influenced.
metaTags.placement: Within `[vocals]` or `[structure]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   solo – single vocalist scat improvisation.

metaTags.name: Scherzo
metaTags.meaning: Defines a lively, fast-paced musical section, often humorous or energetic.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], [theme], or [rhythm]. Accepted Parameters: playful - Light, whimsical feel.

metaTags.name: Secondary theme
metaTags.meaning: Defines a contrasting melody or motif introduced after the main theme.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [theme], [development], or [structure]. Accepted Parameters: contrasting - Distinctly different from the main theme.

metaTags.name: Sequence
metaTags.meaning: Specifies the order and repetition of musical sections, helping structure the track dynamically.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], ensuring that segments appear in a defined order. Accepted Parameters: linear - A straightforward progression of sections.

metaTags.name: Sforzando
metaTags.meaning: Indicates a sudden, strong accent on a note or chord, creating dramatic impact.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [dynamics] or [harmony], marking specific sections that need extra emphasis. Accepted Parameters: single-hit - One strong, immediate accent.

metaTags.name: Sfx
metaTags.meaning: Inserts non-musical sound effects, such as ambient noises, environmental recordings, or mechanical sounds.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [effects], [mixing], or [atmosphere]. Accepted Parameters: wind - Background wind or stormy gusts.

metaTags.name: Shout
metaTags.meaning: Defines a shouted or forcefully spoken vocal element, typically for intensity or emphasis.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [vocals], [effects], or [background-vocals]. Accepted Parameters: single - One loud, emphasized shout.

metaTags.name: Signal Processing
metaTags.meaning: Defines digital or analog audio effects applied to instruments, vocals, or the entire mix to enhance or alter the sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[effects]`, `[mixing]`, or `[instruments]`. Accepted Parameters: reverb - Adds depth and space to the sound.

metaTags.name: Silence
metaTags.meaning: Inserts intentional moments of complete silence into the track, often used for dramatic effect or transitions.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], [dynamics], or [effects], often in conjunction with [drop], [break], or [pause] to create contrast. Accepted Parameters: short - A brief moment of silence, lasting milliseconds to a few seconds.

metaTags.name: Sincopation
metaTags.meaning: Defines off-beat rhythmic accents, often creating a more dynamic and unpredictable groove.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [rhythm], [pulse], or [structure] to alter the beat pattern. Accepted Parameters: light - Subtle syncopation, slightly off-grid but smooth.

metaTags.name: Solo
metaTags.meaning: Highlights a single instrument or voice performing an exposed lead passage within the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [instrument] or [bridge] to indicate a featured instrumental performance. Accepted Parameters: guitar - A classic electric or acoustic guitar solo.

metaTags.name: Sonority
metaTags.meaning: Describes the overall tonal quality and richness of a sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[tone]`, or `[mixing]`. Accepted Parameters: bright - Crisp, high-frequency emphasis.

metaTags.name: Spoken word
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a section tag: `[spoken word | intimate, close-mic, almost whispered]`

metaTags.name: Staccato
metaTags.meaning: Specifies short, detached musical notes, often used for rhythmic sharpness.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [harmony] or [rhythm]. Accepted Parameters: sharp - Strong, abrupt articulation.

metaTags.name: Start
metaTags.meaning: Specifies how the track begins, controlling the intro's structure and impact.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before [intro], defining the opening. Accepted Parameters: sudden - An immediate, high-energy start.

metaTags.name: Stereo
metaTags.meaning: Controls the stereo width and spatial balance of the track, affecting how instruments and sounds are positioned in the left-right stereo field.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before [mixing] or [effects], but can also appear independently to guide the track’s overall stereo imaging. Accepted Parameters: narrow - Focused, centered mix with little stereo spread.

metaTags.name: Stretto
metaTags.meaning: A contrapuntal technique where overlapping melodic phrases occur in close succession, commonly found in fugues and polyphonic compositions.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[counterpoint]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: tight - Very close entries of the theme.

metaTags.name: Structure
metaTags.meaning: Defines the overall song arrangement, specifying how sections like intro, verse, chorus, and bridge are organized.
metaTags.placement: Before structural meta-tags (intro, verse, chorus, etc.), outlining their sequence. Accepted Parameters: standard - Conventional verse-chorus form.

metaTags.name: Style
metaTags.meaning: Defines the musical style or aesthetic of the track, influencing genre fusion and overall production approach.
metaTags.placement: Before [genre] or [mood], specifying artistic direction. Accepted Parameters: minimalist - Sparse instrumentation, subtle textures.

metaTags.name: Subject
metaTags.meaning: Marks the primary musical theme or motif, especially in fugues or structured classical/jazz works.
metaTags.placement: Within `[harmony]`, `[melody]`, or `[structure]`. *   Accepted Parameters: *   main – introduces the principal theme.

metaTags.name: Subharmonic
metaTags.meaning: Refers to frequencies below the fundamental pitch, often used to enhance low-end power.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[bass]`, `[mixing]`, or `[instruments]`. Accepted Parameters: deep - Emphasized low-end, rumbling bass.

metaTags.name: Sustain
metaTags.meaning: Defines how long a note or sound is held before fading.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[dynamics]`, `[instruments]`, or `[mixing]`. Accepted Parameters: long - Notes are held for a prolonged period.

metaTags.name: Swell
metaTags.meaning: Defines a gradual increase in volume or intensity, often leading into climactic sections.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before [dynamics], [chorus], or [build-up], signaling increasing energy. Accepted Parameters: orchestral - Expanding instrumentation, often with strings or brass.

metaTags.name: Syncopation
metaTags.meaning: Defines offbeat rhythmic emphasis that disrupts a regular pattern.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[rhythm]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: funky - Offbeat groove, typical in funk and jazz.

metaTags.name: Technique
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As part of a section descriptor for arrangement: `[intro | guitar, technique: tremolo picking over reverb]`

metaTags.name: Tempo
metaTags.meaning: Defines the speed (BPM) and pacing of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically before [rhythm] or [mood], influencing groove and feel. Accepted Parameters: slow - Relaxed, chill pacing (BPM 60-90).

metaTags.name: Tension Release
metaTags.meaning: Defines the contrast between musical tension and resolution, essential for emotional impact.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[structure]`, or `[dynamics]`. Accepted Parameters: gradual - Slow buildup of tension leading to resolution.

metaTags.name: Tenuto
metaTags.meaning: Marks notes or phrases that should be sustained for their full value, often giving them expressive weight.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [dynamics] or [articulation], indicating emphasis on certain musical phrases. Accepted Parameters: soft - Gentle sustain with delicate phrasing.

metaTags.name: Tessitura
metaTags.meaning: Defines the average vocal or instrumental pitch range within a composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[vocals]` or `[orchestration]`. Accepted Parameters: low - Notes are concentrated in the lower range.

metaTags.name: Texture
metaTags.meaning: Defines the density and layering of sound within the composition.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [style], [mixing], or [harmony], describing how elements are combined. Accepted Parameters: thin - Sparse, minimal instrumentation.

metaTags.name: Theme
metaTags.meaning: Establishes a recurring melodic, rhythmic, or harmonic idea, serving as the track’s central motif.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], guiding how the main idea develops. Accepted Parameters: primary - The main recurring motif.

metaTags.name: Timbre
metaTags.meaning: Describes the unique tonal color or character of a sound.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[mixing]`, `[tone]`, or `[orchestration]`. Accepted Parameters: bright - Sharp, crisp overtones.

metaTags.name: Tone
metaTags.meaning: Defines the overall timbral character of the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before [mood] or [style], shaping the emotional and sonic color. Accepted Parameters: bright - Crisp, high-frequency emphasis.

metaTags.name: Tone Cluster
metaTags.meaning: A dense grouping of notes played simultaneously, creating dissonance or texture.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]` or `[structure]`. Accepted Parameters: soft - Light, atmospheric clusters.

metaTags.name: Transition
metaTags.meaning: Defines how sections of the track connect, shaping smooth or dramatic changes.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], [tempo], or [dynamics], influencing movement between parts. Accepted Parameters: smooth - Gradual shift between sections.

metaTags.name: Tremolo
metaTags.meaning: Defines a rapid repetition of a note or oscillation in pitch to create intensity, tension, or vibrato effects.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [harmony] or [dynamics] to specify expressive playing techniques. Accepted Parameters: soft - Light tremolo, subtle and delicate.

metaTags.name: Trio
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a three-part instrumental or vocal arrangement, commonly used in classical music but applicable to modern settings.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [ensemble], [harmony], or [vocals] to define the arrangement of performers. Accepted Parameters: classical - Traditional trio structure (e.g., piano, violin, cello).

metaTags.name: Variation
metaTags.meaning: Specifies a modified version of a previous musical section, used to develop and evolve themes.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [theme] or [structure] to guide motif transformations. Accepted Parameters: melodic - Variation in melody while retaining the harmonic framework.

metaTags.name: Verse
metaTags.meaning: Defines a repeated song section with evolving lyrics or instrumentation, typically alternating with a chorus.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within [structure], indicating lyrical or instrumental sections. Accepted Parameters: soft - Gentle and subdued verse dynamics.

metaTags.name: Vibe
metaTags.meaning: Defines the general feel, groove, and aesthetic of the track, guiding mood and instrumentation.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before [mood] or [style], influencing the overall musical atmosphere. Accepted Parameters: chill - Relaxed, laid-back energy.

metaTags.name: Vocalist
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* At the top of the track, or before the first sung section: `[vocalist | soft female voice, indie folk tone]`

metaTags.name: Vocal Style
metaTags.meaning: Suggests vocal performance character — texture, mood, or technique.
metaTags.placement: Use within `[vocals:]` OR inline with `[verse:]`, `[chorus:]`, etc. Most effective in emotionally charged or stylized genres. Accepted Parameters:

metaTags.name: Vocals
metaTags.meaning: Defines the presence, type, and characteristics of vocal performances in the track.
metaTags.placement: Typically used before [structure] or [style], guiding the vocal performance and its prominence. Accepted Parameters: lead - Primary vocal line leading the melody.

metaTags.name: Voicing
metaTags.meaning: Defines how notes are distributed across instruments or voices, affecting chord texture and balance.
metaTags.placement: Typically used within `[harmony]`, `[orchestration]`, or `[mixing]`. Accepted Parameters: open - Notes are spread out over a large range.

metaTags.name: Vulnerable vocals
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* At the start of emotionally raw sections: `[verse | vulnerable vocals, soft, nearly breaking]`

metaTags.name: Whisper
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a local delivery tag: `[whisper] the secrets only night can hear`

metaTags.name: Whispering
metaTags.placement: *Lyrics field:* As a background / texture cue, often in brackets or side-notes: `[whispering voices in the background, unintelligible, eerie]`

trademarkSubstitutions.guidance: use "krautrock" / "old school EDM" etc when describing the style/technique
when defining the guitar play style, use "ska stroke" instead

vocalTokens: Specifies a freely improvised vocal or instrumental phrase, often used in solos or embellishments
Specifies harmonized or complementary vocals that support the lead vocal
Specifies a repetitive, rhythmic vocal phrase, often used for emphasis or ritualistic effect
Specifies group vocal harmonization, often for a grand, emotional effect
Applies a gritty, overdriven, or processed effect to the voice
Suggests vocal performance character — texture, mood, or technique
Defines the presence, type, and characteristics of vocal performances in the track
productionTokens: Defines the organization of musical elements, including instrumentation, section order, and layering
Specifies how notes are played in terms of attack, transition, and connection between them
Defines how quickly a note reaches its full volume after being played
Defines the bassline characteristics, its prominence, and style
Specifies a sliding bass note, often used for groove or tension
Defines dynamic range processing, used to balance loud and soft parts in a track
Specifies audio processing that adds harmonic saturation, clipping, or fuzz to an instrument or soun
Specifies a short percussive passage that serves as a transition between sections
Defines equalization settings, adjusting frequency balance for clarity or effect
Specifies the level and type of reverberation, controlling depth and space in the track’s mix
Specifies a strong and continuous reverb effect, creating an echoing, immersive atmosphere
Controls the stereo width and spatial balance of the track, affecting how instruments and sounds are
Defines the speed (BPM) and pacing of the track
principles: Style field: use 4–8 strong tags; put instrumental-only guards last when present (v5.5 community guidance).
Lyrics field: bracketed section tags + pipe overrides for local changes — e.g. [chorus | style: hook, vocals: autotune-light].
Open with [track: genre:, mood:, length:, instruments:] for global defaults when building long prompts.
Avoid protected trademark names in Style — use descriptive substitutes (see trademarkSubstitutions in sync manifest).
Parameterized tags beat vague adjectives — [verse: soft, intimate vocal lines over acoustic guitar].
Trademark guard: e.g. "kraftwerk" → use "krautrock" / "old school EDM" etc when describing the style/technique

pipeNotation.example: [chorus | style: phonk hook, vocals: autotune-light, melodic]

trackContainerTag.example: [track: genre: phonk drift, style: lo-fi hip-hop, mood: gritty night drive, length: 180, instruments: 808 sub-bass, vinyl crackle]
trackContainerTag.note: Place at the very top of the lyrics field before section tags.
